SFTD.Warning: Be aware, this will clear all Pressure/Hazard
SFTD.CrewTypeBox: Click to choose a Crew Type
SFTD.ChooseOneOrTwoEdges: Choose one or two edges and an equal number of flaws
SFTD.SpecialistInformation: When a Specialist performs actions for which their specialties apply, they use their full Quality rating, otherwise their Quality is zero.
SFTD.ClassExpClue1: Every time you roll a desperate action, mark xp in that action's attribute.
SFTD.ClassExpClue2: You expressed your history, background, or anchors.
SFTD.ClassExpClue3: You struggled with issues from your belief or scars.
SFTD.ClassExpClueDescription: At the end of each session, for each item below, mark 1 xp (in your playbook or an attribute) or 2 xp if that item occurred multiple times.
SFTD.CrewExpClue1: At the end of each session, for each item below, mark 1 xp (or instead mark 2xp if that item occurred multiple times).

Independent.Label: Independent
Independent.Description: The specialist can be trusted to make good decisions and act on their own initiative in the absence of direct instruction.

Unrelenting.Label: Unrelenting
Unrelenting.Description: The specialist won't be deterred from a task.

Loyal.Label: Loyal
Loyal.Description: The specialist can't be bribed or turned against you.

Sociable.Label: Sociable
Sociable.Description: The specialist is generally well-liked and well-regarded.

Nimble.Label: Nimble
Nimble.Description: The vehicle handles easily. Consider this an assist (take +1d if a PC rolls) for tricky maneuvers.

Auto-Repair.Label: Auto-Repair
Auto-Repair.Description: The vehicle self-repairs. Remove all of its harm during downtime.

Rugged.Label: Rugged
Rugged.Description: The vehicle keeps operating even when it's paralyzed.

Unreliable.Label: Unreliable
Unreliable.Description: The specialist isn't always available due to other obligations.

Ill-liked.Label: Ill-liked
Ill-liked.Description: The specialist is unpopular and disliked for some reason.

Principled.Label: Principled
Principled.Description: The specialist has a core ethic or values that they won't betray.

Reckless.Label: Reckless
Reckless.Description: The specialist is unpredictable and given to rash action.

Guzzler.Label: Guzzler
Guzzler.Description: The vehicle requires 1 shell's worth of fuel per downtime to keep in operation.

Finicky.Label: Finicky
Finicky.Description: The vehicle has quirks that only one person understands. Without them, the vehicle has -1 Quality.

Flashy.Label: Flashy
Flashy.Description: The vehicle is ostentatious and very memorable. Take +1 pressure when you use it on a mission.

SFTD.HarmImpairedDescription: The specialist operates with reduced quality (-1d).
SFTD.HarmBrokenDescription: The specialist can't do anything until they recover.
SFTD.HarmDeadDescription: The specialist is destroyed. You can spend coin equal to your Tier +2 to restore it, plus two downtime activities to recruit new gang members, or hire a new expert.
SFTD.RollPartialSuccessPositionControlled: You hesitate. Withdraw and try a different approach, or else do it with a minor consequence: a minor complication occurs, you have reduced impact, you suffer lesser harm, you end up in a risky position.
SFTD.RollPartialSuccessPositionRisky: You do it, but there's a consequence: you suffer harm, a complication occurs, you have reduced impact, you end up in a desperate position.
SFTD.RollPartialSuccessPosition: You do it, but there's a consequence: you suffer severe harm, a serious complication occurs, you have reduced impact.
SFTD.RollFailurePositionControlled: You falter. Press on by seizing a risky opportunity, or withdraw and try a different approach.
SFTD.RollFailurePositionRisky: Things go badly. You suffer harm, a complication occurs, you end up in a desperate position, you lose this opportunity.
SFTD.RollFailurePositionDesperate: It's the worst outcome. You suffer severe harm, a serious complication occurs, you lose this opportunity for action.
SFTD.RollDoWithIncreasedImpactSpecialist: The specialist does it with increased impact.
SFTD.RollResistance:
You reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence (GM chooses).
Suffer {stress} stress.
SFTD.RollResistanceCritical:
You reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence (GM chooses).
Clear 1 stress.
SFTD.RollThreatPeril: Suffer a reduced consequence or push yourself to avoid the threat.
SFTD.RollThreatThreat: You suffer the consequence If you push yourself, reduce the consequence.
SFTD.ActionsAnalysisInfo: Analysis is the act of detailed observation. It reflects your mental focus.
SFTD.ActionsDecipherInfo: When you Decipher, you use fine details to figure out people, systems, and secrets.
SFTD.ActionsShapeInfo: When you Shape, you alter existing technology or craft new things from disparate parts.
SFTD.ActionsSunderInfo: When you Sunder, you find weak points and break them open.
SFTD.ActionsTraceInfo: When you Trace, you carefully track targets or search for hidden things.
SFTD.ActionsKinesisInfo: Kinesis is the act of physical motion.
SFTD.ActionsClashInfo: When you Clash, you engage in pitched and open combat.
SFTD.ActionsHandleInfo: When you Handle, you employ dexterity, deftness, or subtle skill.
SFTD.ActionsShadowInfo: When you Shadow, you move quickly and quietly.
SFTD.ActionsWayfareInfo: When you Wayfare, you explore safely and traverse skillfully.
SFTD.ActionsSemiosisInfo: Semiosis is the act of making meaning.
SFTD.ActionsBluffInfo: When you Bluff, you manipulate with charm, deception, or fast talk.
SFTD.ActionsCommandInfo: When you Command, you employ good leadership or compel by force of personality.
SFTD.ActionsConnectInfo: When you Connect, you socialize with friends and contacts, form sincere emotional bonds, or persuade from a position of genuine belief.
SFTD.ActionsTuneInfo: When you Tune, you align your senses to the transcendent forces that underlie the material world.
SFTD.ForcePosition: Force?
SFTD.ForceImpact: Force?
SFTD.AddFoundationProject: Adds the Foundation '{foundation}' once the project is complete.
SFTD.EffectWarning: Managing Active Effects within an Owned Item is not currently supported by FoundryVTT and will be added in a subsequent update.
SFTD.Name: Name
SFTD.HarmonyClue:
Tick and untick petals to track your crew's harmony. You can spend harmony one-for-one on an action roll to gain a bonus die for that roll.

You generate harmony when you roll a 6 or critical on a desperate action or on any teamwork maneuver: a group action, a roll that was assisted, a roll to set someone up or that was set up, a resistance roll to protect someone.
SFTD.InvestedCacheInfo: This cache has been invested. It cannot be removed.
SFTD.AcquireAssetExtra: To raise the result beyond critical, you may spend 2 coin per +1 Tier bonus.
SFTD.AftermathRollFull: Aftermath
SFTD.UpkeepRollFull: Upkeep
SFTD.CollectDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a known Region here to set it as the Region to collect from.
SFTD.SchmoozeDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a known Faction here to set it as the Faction to improve relationship with.
SFTD.UpkeepDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a known Faction here to set it as the Faction to gain supplies from.
SFTD.TargetFactionDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a Faction here to set it as the Faction who was the target for that mission.
SFTD.EmployerFactionDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a Faction here to set it as the Faction who issued that mission to the crew.
SFTD.LeaderTextStress: The leader of this group action, {leader}, suffered {stress} Stress due to failed actions.
SFTD.LeaderTextQuirk: The leader of this group action, {leader}, expends {quirks} Quirk(s) due to failed actions.
SFTD.RollCollectInformationSuccess: Strong: You find even more than you were looking for. The information might reveal secrets or opportunities you hadn't known about. Definitely ask a follow-up question or two, the MC will give you a good answer.
SFTD.RollCollectInformationPartialSuccess: Normal: You've got solid details. You might be able to ask a follow-up question or two to clarify.
SFTD.RollCollectInformationFailure: Weak: You get the bare basics, but not much else. You're probably missing key details.
SFTD.RollEngagementCritical: Exceptional result. You've already overcome the first obstacle and you're in a controlled position for what's next.
SFTD.RollEngagementSuccess: Good result. You're in a controlled position when the action starts.
SFTD.RollEngagementPartialSuccess: Mixed result. You're in a risky position when the action starts.
SFTD.RollEngagementFailure: Bad result. You're in a desperate position when the action starts.
SFTD.RollFortuneCritical: The result is exceptional. You have strong or extreme impact.
SFTD.RollFortuneSuccess: The result is good. You have standard or full impact.
SFTD.RollFortunePartialSuccess: The result is mixed. You have weak or partial impact.
SFTD.RollFortuneFailure: The result is bad. You have poor or little impact.

1.Title: Windfall
1.Description: Someone discovers a cache of ancient materials and tech. The first time you roll this aftermath for a community, you can choose any Prosperity 4 foundation: it counts as Prosperity 1 for you. On subsequent windfalls, your community gets 2 shells.

2.Title: Lucky Break
2.Description: One of you stumbles across an opportunity for a well-paying mission. Take +1d when you roll for upkeep at the end of your next job.

3.Title: Quintessent Life
3.Description: A magical creature or strange spirit begins to cause havoc in your community. Acquire the services of a charmant to banish or calm it or figure out how to do the same yourselves.

4.Title: Weather Damage
4.Description: Harsh weather or a minor natural danger strikes your community. Lose a foundation until you pay half its cost in shells to repair it (round up). Paying these costs doesn't use up a downtime activity.

5.Title: Community Conflict
5.Description: A fight breaks out in your community. Head it off, shut it down, or spend shells equal to your Prosperity to repair the damage. If you can't defuse the tension successfully, take +2 pressure.

6.Title: Escalation
6.Description: Roll with -1d on the next highest hazard list.

1.Title: Discovery
1.Description: Someone makes a discovery (a piece of buried Radiant tech, a new use for a magical plant, etc.) that proves tremendously useful. Divide 3 ticks between any number of Foundation or Long-Term Project clocks, and say how the discovery helped those projects advance.

2.Title: Quintessent Life
2.Description: A magical creature or strange spirit begins to cause havoc in your community. Acquire the services of a charmant to banish or calm it or figure out how to do the same yourselves.

3.Title: A Stranger Walks Into Town
3.Description: A stranger walks into town with what seems like an incredible mission offer, but there's something they're holding back. If you run the mission, it pays +2 shells, but you take 2 pressure immediately, and the MC will give the mission an additional secret complication.

4.Title: Community Conflict
4.Description: A fight breaks out in your community. Head it off, shut it down, or spend shells equal to your Prosperity to repair the damage. If you can't defuse the tension successfully, take +2 pressure.

5.Title: Spacetime Scream
5.Description: Strange visions scream out across the fabric of reality, piercing into the minds of your crew. Resist with Semiosis or take Level 1 Harm as the visions tear through you. (You still see the visions even if you resist, they just don't cause you pain.)

6.Title: Escalation
6.Description: Roll with -1d on the next highest hazard list.

1.Title: A Stranger Walks Into Town
1.Description: A stranger walks into town with what seems like an incredible mission offer, but there's something they're holding back. If you run the mission, it pays +2 shells, but you take 2 pressure immediately, and the MC will give the mission an additional secret complication.

2.Title: Radiant Eruption
2.Description: A piece of long-dormant technology buried nearby suddenly comes alive, with strange effects on your community and the nearby landscape. The effects are chaotic and disruptive, but if you can figure out how to safely disable or control the haywire tech, you might be able to reverse-engineer some of it.

3.Title: Spacetime Scream
3.Description: Strange visions scream out across the fabric of reality, piercing into the minds of your crew. Resist with Semiosis or take Level 1 Harm as the visions tear through you. (You still see the visions even if you resist, they just don't cause you pain.)

4.Title: Malfunctions
4.Description: Some important piece of machinery in your community breaks down. Each strider will be asked to use up 1 downtime activity on repairs, take 1 pressure per Strider who ignores this task. Untick all Project and Foundation clocks by 1 to account for technical setbacks or pay shells equal to your Prosperity to preserve them.

5.Title: Trouble-Seekers
5.Description: A rival faction is looking for trouble and sends a detachment to your community with a ridiculous set of demands. Figure out how to meet them, bribe the detachment to get them off your back (shells equal to the Faction's Tier), or refuse them and take 2 pressure and -1 status with the faction. If you have no negative statuses with any factions, you avoid aftermaths for now.

6.Title: Escalation
6.Description: Roll with -1d on the next highest hazard list.

1.Title: Dangerous Mission
1.Description: Someone has come to your crew with an exceptionally dangerous opportunity. If you take the offer, you take -1d to the engagement roll for this mission and the MC will introduce a hidden complication, but you get +3 shells at upkeep.

2.Title: High-Profile Op
2.Description: An allied faction or community urgently needs your help. They're willing to pay well, but it's a high-profile job. If you take the mission, you get +2 shells at payout, but every time you take pressure on the job, take +1 pressure. If you don't take the mission, lose 1 status with your ally. If you have no positive statuses with any factions, you avoid aftermaths for now.

3.Title: Zealous Rival
3.Description: A rival or enemy makes a hard move against one of your crew or someone they're close to. Let them hurt you or yours, allow them to extract a promise from you, or stop them some other way. If they successfully cause chaos in your community, +3 pressure.

4.Title: Disaster
4.Description: A disaster (natural or engineered) endangers your community. Lose three Foundations (MC's choice) until you repair them. Each foundation requires twice its cost in shells to be repaired. Paying these costs (or averting the disaster another way) doesn't consume a downtime activity.

5.Title: Crisis
5.Description: A nearby disaster has put innocent people in danger, and your expertise is needed ASAP. If you take the mission, you get -2d to the Engagement roll. If you don't, then the suffering of those people is on your conscience and your reputation: take 4 pressure and everyone in the crew takes the Level 1 Harm: Guilty (which can be resisted as normal).

6.Title: Invasion
6.Description: It's the worst outcome. One or a group of your enemies marshals an army and invades with minimal warning. Your next mission must be dealing with that invasion. You don't get downtime activities until the next mission ends. Any invading faction(s) that don't already have a vendetta against you gain one. Reset to Hazard 3 with 0 pressure after this occurs.

SFTD.RollAftermathCantHateUs: Your crew really can't be hated by outsiders. Roll a second aftermath and pick the one you prefer among the two.
SFTD.RollAcquireAssetResultSuccess: You gain an asset of Tier {tier_quality} quality.
SFTD.RollAcquireAssetResultFailure: It was unfortunately not enough. You could not gain an asset of Tier {success_tier} quality or above. If you want to acquire it anyway, since your current asset is of Tier {tier_quality} quality, you need to spend {materiel} Materiel in total.
SFTD.RollAcquireAssetExtra: To raise the result of this roll, you may spend 1 Materiel per Tier level, or raise it beyond critical success by spending 2 Materiel per additional level added.
SFTD.RollCutLoose:
You cut loose with {strider}.
You cleared {clear_stress} Stress.
SFTD.RollCutLooseSavedByCarouse: Your carousing lowered your Stress recovery, but prevented you from overindulging.
SFTD.RollCutLooseCarouseStress: Your carousing lowered your Stress recovery.
SFTD.RollCutLooseSavedByConviction: You gathered a significant data set, and didn't overindulge thanks to your focus on experimentation.
SFTD.RollCutLooseConviction: Your focus on experimentation helped you gather a significant data set.
SFTD.RollCutLooseFunctioningVice: Your functioning vice allows you to manually adjust the amount of stress recovered by up to +2 or -2.
SFTD.RollCutLooseSavedByFunctioningVice: Your functioning vice prevented you from overindulging by {num}, since it allows you to manually adjust the amount of stress recovered by up to +2 or -2.
SFTD.RollCutLooseDoubleFailure: You cut loose with {strider} and both of you overindulge. You cleared {clear_stress} Stress and choose one of the following consequences which apply to both Striders:
SFTD.RollCutLooseFailure: You cut loose with {strider} and overindulge. You cleared {clear_stress} Stress and choose one of the following consequences:
SFTD.RollCutLooseFailureAttactTrouble: Attract Trouble. Roll an additional Aftermath using the table and dice from the end of the previous mission.
SFTD.RollCutLooseFailureBrag: Brag about your exploits. Lose 2 Trust with your Patron Faction.
SFTD.RollCutLooseFailureLost: AWOL. Your Strider vanishes for a few weeks. Play a different Strider until this one returns from their 'vacation'. When your Strider returns, they've also healed all their Harm.
SFTD.RollCutLooseFailureTapped: Impropriety. Make an ass out of yourself in front of the Strider that joined you-reset your Connection Clock with them to 0. If your Connection is already 0, take the Level 1 Harm: Alone.
SFTD.RollCutLooseExtraResult: You may spend 1 Materiel to increase the result by 1.
SFTD.RollCutLooseConnectionCarouseStrider: Your bond with {strider} grew a lot thanks to your carousing.
SFTD.RollCutLooseConnectionCarouseOther: {strider}'s bond with you grew thanks to your carousing.
SFTD.RollCutLooseConnectionMaxxed: Your Connection Clock is full. Ask {strider} for a truth about one belief you have about them. Your Connection Clock with them has been reset to 1 Tick as you see them in a new light. You both take 1 XP, which can go into any XP track.
SFTD.RollCutLooseConnectionCarouseOtherMaxxed: {strider}'s Connection Clock is full. {strider} asks for a truth about one belief they have about you. Their Connection Clock with you has been reset to 1 Tick as they see you in a new light. You both take 1 XP, which can go into any XP track.
SFTD.RollCutLooseBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Cut Loose activity if you so wish.
SFTD.RollCutLooseOtherStrider: Roll Cut Loose for the other Strider
SFTD.RollLongTermProject: You spent some time working on the Long-Term Project {ltp}. Its Progress Clock was filled by {tick} tick(s).
SFTD.RollLongTermProjectIronsInTheFire: You spent some time working on Long-Term Projects {ltps}. {tick} tick(s) were distributed as evenly as possible across their Progress Clocks.
SFTD.RollLongTermProjectIronsInTheFireRemaind: {tick} tick(s) could not be automatically distributed. Pick which Long-Term Project(s) they should be assigned to, with a max of one tick per project.
SFTD.RollLongTermProjectIronsInTheFireOverTic: {tick} tick(s) could not be distributed because all projects were completed.
SFTD.RollLongTermProjectExtraResult: You may spend 1 Personnel to increase the roll's result by 1.
SFTD.RollLongTermProjectBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Long-Term Project activity if you so wish.
SFTD.RollManufactureResultSuccess: You manufacture an item of Tier {tier_quality} quality.
SFTD.RollManufactureResultFailure: It was unfortunately not enough. You could not manufacture an item of Tier {success_tier} quality or above. If you want to manufacture it anyway, since your current item is of Tier {tier_quality} quality, you need to spend {materiel} Materiel in total.
SFTD.RollManufactureExtra: To raise the result of this roll, you may spend 1 Materiel per Tier level, even beyond critical success.
SFTD.RollRecover: You spent some time resting to treat your wounds.
SFTD.RollRecoverLevelOneHarm: All Level 1 Harm has been cleared.
SFTD.RollRecoverResultFull: All Harm has been reduced by one level.
SFTD.RollSchmooze: You improved your crew {crew}'s relationship with the faction {faction} by {trust} Trust.
SFTD.RollSchmoozeBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Schmooze activity if you so wish.
SFTD.TrainText: You spend some time training {trainTypeDesc}. Your {trainTypeText} XP was increased by {num}.
SFTD.TrainLevelUpPlaybook: Your XP track has been filled. Take a new Playbook special ability.
SFTD.TrainLevelUpAttribute: Your XP track has been filled. Add an Action Point under the {trainType} attribute.
SFTD.RollMoveBase: You spent some time relocating your mobile FOB to a new location.
SFTD.DowntimeExtraResult: You may spend up to {num} Shells to improve the result level, at the cost of 1 Shell per result level.
SFTD.CollectionAgencyContents: Your collection agency has collected {value} Materiel for you.
SFTD.SideBusinessContents: Your side business has collected {value} Personnel for you.
SFTD.StayLateInfo:
You may provide a crewmate an extra Downtime Activity at the cost of taking the Level 1 Harm: Tired.
Choose the crewmate who will benefit from an extra Downtime Activity below.
SFTD.StayLateDescription: {target} can take an extra Downtime Activity. {actor} takes the Level 1 Harm: Tired, which may be resisted as normal.
SFTD.LotusBargainEffect: You took a Lotus Bargain. Its consequences are unavoidable, nor can they be reduced or negated with a resistance roll or armor.
SFTD.PushYourselfEffect: You pushed yourself to {effect}. You suffered 2 Stress.
SFTD.AssistEffect: {strider} assisted you. They suffered 1 Stress. You have gained +1d.
SFTD.DowntimeAssistOtherEffect: An ally or your direct superior assisted you. It is likely to provide access to resources otherwise unavailable.
SFTD.SetupEffect: {strider} set you up for success. Your {effect} has improved.
SFTD.SettingUpEffect: You are setting something up for {strider}. They can choose to increase their Position or Impact on their next roll.
SFTD.ProtectEffect: You've protected {strider} from harm, directing it toward you instead.
SFTD.DisabledByAE: Minimum Value set by an Active Effect
SFTD.StartMissionDescription:
This popup allows you to automate some parts of the mission starting process.
Any ticked checkbox will let the system automate that part of the process to the best of its ability.
This also serves as a cheat sheet: only green options have automation linked to them.
SFTD.StartMissionPrepareMission:
The group covers the mission's target, objective, employer, loadout. They also come up with an approach and its detail.
Its detail is the element that makes the approach work. If you don't know the detail, you can collect information in some way to figure one out.
  • Studied: Use scholarship, intelligence, and lore. Detail: How do you use archival or collected knowledge to your advantage?
  • Curious: Investigate, experiment, discover. Detail: How do you seek out new information?
  • Patient: Be slow, thoughtful, and thorough. Detail: How do you make use of the extra time you spend?
  • Rapid: Act fast. Detail: How do you gain the speed advantage?
  • Subtle: Act without notice or pass without trace. Detail: How do you minimize your presence?
  • Brazen: Command attention, make bold moves. Detail: How do you maximize your presence?
SFTD.StartMissionNoCutLooseScar:
If any Strider has one or several Scars and did not take a Cut Loose Downtime Activity,
they take Stress equal to the number of Scars they have,
unless they got a new Scar in the last mission.
Deselect the Striders who got a Scar in the last mission!
SFTD.StartMissionEngagement: The Striders make an Engagement roll to move into the mission.
SFTD.StartMissionNoCutLooseScarEffect: Some Striders with scars have not taken a Cut Loose activity:
SFTD.StartMissionRecoverMelody: All Striders have recovered their melody.
SFTD.StartMissionStart: The Mission starts now. Good luck!
SFTD.EndMissionDescription:
This popup allows you to automate some parts of the mission ending process.
Any ticked checkbox will let the system automate that part of the process to the best of its ability.
This also serves as a cheat sheet: only green options have automation linked to them.
For anything related to Pressure Gain, enabling a bullet point means its effect is applied. Only enable the ones that apply to the mission!
NOTE! NONE OF THE AUTOMATION OPTIONS BELOW ARE READY YET
SFTD.EndMissionThread:
Choose a thread. A thread is a question you can use to decide what themes or aspects of the community
you want to explore during this downtime. You don't have to make sure everything you do during downtime
relates to the thread, and you don't need to have a definitive answer by the end of the downtime session.
Just keep your thread in mind while you play through your downtime,
and integrate the topic when it seems appropriate or interesting.
SFTD.EndMissionUpkeepRoll:
If the mission was successful, take an Upkeep Roll from the employer Faction.
(Must be rolled through the Misc. Rolls menu on the Strider sheet).
SFTD.EndMissionPressureCollateralDamageMajor: Chaotic; problem spills over into civilian areas; major collateral damage; major acts of violence.
SFTD.EndMissionPressureCollateralDamageDevast:
Wild and devastating; the problem majorly intrudes into civilian areas;
severe collateral damage; there are civilian casualties.
SFTD.EndMissionPressureHighProfileTarget: Mission was conducted against a high-profile or well-connected target?
SFTD.EndMissionPressureVendetta:
Add Pressure for each Faction that holds a vendetta against you.
Current Faction(s) in vendetta with you: {vendettas}
SFTD.EndMissionPressureSevereInjury: Were people severely injured or killed on the mission?
SFTD.EndMissionPressureSevereInjuryYourFault: Was anyone in your crew responsible for such?
SFTD.EndMissionAftermath:
You must roll an Aftermath, as nothing happens in isolation.
(Roll it from the Misc. Rolls menu on the Strider sheet AFTER this process is completed so Pressure and Hazard are up to date).
SFTD.EndMissionDowntimeStart: The Mission has ended, Striders may now partake in Downtime Activities.
SFTD.EndSessionDescription:
This sheet has no automation linked to it. It is merely just a cheat sheet for handling the end of a session.
Feel free to untick tasks you've already done as you go through them with your players!
SFTD.EndSessionXP:
Each Strider reviews the XP triggers on their Strider sheet. For each one, mark 1 XP if it happened at all and 2 XP
if it happened multiple times during the session. The XP triggers are:
  • Your playbook-specific XP trigger. For example, the Aegis' XP trigger is
    “Address a challenge with endurance or intercept danger before it strikes.”
    You don't necessarily need to succeed at or overcome the challenge,
    as long as you're engaging an obstacle with endurance or intercepting danger, you mark the XP.
  • Express your history, background, or anchors. This is deliberately very broad!
    Whenever you feel that these aspects of your character were highlighted, you can mark XP for them.
  • Struggle with issues from your belief or scars.
    Mark XP for this if your belief or scars caused trouble for you or your crew.
You can attribute said XP in your playbook track, any of your attribute tracks or your crew track.
SFTD.EndSessionMissionComplete:
If a mission was completed this session, ask the Striders the following questions to direct what the next mission will be:
  • Are you following up on a previous mission's outcome?
  • Is your aim to aid yourselves, or hurt/aid another community?
  • Are you targeting a community weaker, stronger, or about the same as you?
SFTD.EndSessionMentalCheck:
Check on each player's emotional state. Sometimes sessions can get very intense,
and players don't always realize how affected they are until they take a break.
If you're concerned about another player who says they're alright,
consider asking them privately in a couple of days once they've had a chance to assess their emotional state.
SFTD.EndSessionLikeDislikeCheck:
Ask the players what they liked and disliked about the session, and what they want to see more or less of in future sessions.
If players can't think of anything, try asking in a couple of days after they've all had some time to sit with the session.
SFTD.EndSessionLinesVeils: Remind people about Lines and Veils if needed, and let them know they can update those lists whenever they like.
SFTD.MultipleSelectUsage: Ctrl + Click to select several choices

Action.Name: Action Rolls
Action.Hint: Enables the Action Roll from the Actor Dice Roller; only uncheck if you have enabled Threat Rolls.

Threat.Name: Threat Rolls
Threat.Hint: Enables the Threat Roll from the Deep Cuts supplement.

Push.Name: Push Yourself Rolls
Push.Hint: Replaces the base Resistance Roll with the Push Yourself Roll from the Deep Cuts supplement.

Load.Name: Alternate Load
Load.Hint: Replaces the base Load Rules (Light/Normal/Heavy) with the Load Rules (Discreet/Conspicuous) from the Deep Cuts supplement.

PublicClocks.Name: Default Observable Clocks
PublicClocks.Hint: All newly made Clock actors will have permissions set to Observer for all players.

ClockStyles.Name: Clock Styles
ClockStyles.Label: Configure Clock Styles...
ClockStyles.Hint:
List of all detected clock styles that can be used.
System clock styles are stored in your Foundry folder's systems/songs-for-the-dusk/themes folder.
World clock styles are stored in your Foundry folder's worlds/<YourWorld>/themes folder.
ClockStyles.Fine1: All of the clocks sprites in this
ClockStyles.Fine2: are valid and fine to use
ClockStyles.IsShifted: Is Shifted?
ClockStyles.WrongExtension: Wrong file extension for fill {fill} (found .{bad}, looking for .{good})

DefaultClockThemeColor.Name: Default Clock Style & Color
DefaultClockThemeColor.Hint: Clock style and color used by default in sheets.

DowntimeRules.Name: Downtime Rules
DowntimeRules.Hint:
Whether not having any more Downtime Activities available prevents rolls which use them or not.
Strict: Rolls are prevented.
Lax: They are not.

ActorDragAndDrop.Name: Actor Drag & Drop
ActorDragAndDrop.Hint:
Indicates who is allowed to drag & drop actors. Note that this behavior is used for some rolls and features.
Everyone: Every user is allowed to drag and drop actors.
Trusted Players & GMs: Normal players are forbidden.
All Gamemasters: All players are forbidden.
Gamemaster: Only the main Gamemaster is allowed.

IncrementClock.Name: Increment Clock
IncrementClock.Hint:
Adds 1 tick to all selected clocks if they're not full.
Clocks are maxed if Shift is also pressed.

DecrementClock.Name: Decrement Clock
DecrementClock.Hint:
Rermoves 1 tick from all selected clocks if they're not empty.
Clocks are emptied if Shift is also pressed.

info.SameCrew: The dropped Crew is the current crew of this strider.
